mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held at low levels (currently 1.16%) signals accommodative monetary policy that compresses equity discount rates, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate does not rise materially.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Neither series alone is sufficient; only their co-movement above trend signals a durable expansion.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show forward 60d returns statistically indistinguishable from (or below) unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the single-series RSAFS or INDPRO signal alone explains equivalent variance (interaction adds no incremental Rank IC > 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is simultaneously above 4% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4%, the market prices sustained restrictive policy, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This mechanism is regime-specific to tightening cycles and does not hold when rates are low, as in the current 1.16% snapshot.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: During periods meeting both thresholds, forward 5d returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in housing starts (HOUST) reflects durable consumer demand and construction-sector confidence, which propagates through the broader economy and supports S&P 500 earnings expectations, producing positive forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth below 0.05% signals an early warning of labor-market softening that precedes a short-term equity pullback, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show forward 5d returns not significantly below baseline (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates the central bank tightening into a strengthening supply-side economy, a combination historically associated with late-cycle equity volatility, producing muted or negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this dynamic; the interaction of supply strength and policy tightening is required.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth > 2% AND FEDFUNDS 6-month change > +0.5pp
regime_hint: late-cycle tightening into strong supply growth
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive forward 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (>0.02) over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where FEDFUNDS is elevated, a deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth signals weakening consumer demand under restrictive financing conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). This mechanism is regime-conditional and inactive when rates are low, as in the current snapshot.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: During periods meeting both conditions, forward 60d returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the single RSAFS deceleration signal alone (without the FEDFUNDS condition) produces equivalent predictive power.
